title: Source class: what an outcome claim in agent evidence rests on

canonicalization.value_profile: no floats; integers within +/-2^53; strings and object keys confined to the Basic Multilingual Plane (this corpus is ASCII-only and number-free); for this profile the reference serialization (UTF-8, sorted keys, no whitespace) is byte-equal to RFC 8785 output
canonicalization.note: every digest in this corpus travels with the canonicalization id that produced it; a digest without its scheme id is not comparable and the checker rejects it

values.producer_reported: The party that performed or decided the action also reports the outcome. Signing, hash-chaining, or time-anchoring such a record raises tamper-evidence, never vantage.
values.issuer_attested: A party in the producer's trust domain signs or attests the record. The signature binds the bytes to a key, not the claim to an observation.
values.receiver_receipt: The counterparty that received the action issues the record, for example a called service returning a signed receipt. Independent of the producer, but a party to the exchange.
values.boundary_observed: An interposed chokepoint on the action path (gateway, proxy) records what passed through it. It observes traffic through itself and nothing that bypasses it; the declared boundary is part of the claim.
values.third_party_observed: A party outside both producer and receiver holds the observation in an artifact neither controls, for example a consulted public ledger or a log operated by a neutral party. Naming such an artifact is not consulting it.
values.independently_observed: An observer at a vantage the observed process cannot write to (kernel or host telemetry below the harness) captured the effect.
values.unknown: The basis is declared as not established. This is a typed value a record carries on purpose. It is distinct from a record that says nothing, and a checker treats the two differently.

invariants: declared unknown is distinct from undeclared: a record that says 'basis: unknown' has told the reader something a silent record has not
tamper-evidence never raises vantage: signatures, hash chains, and external time anchors bound when and whether bytes changed, not who could have been wrong about the fact
recomputability never raises vantage: re-deriving a digest confirms the bytes are intact under the declared canonicalization, not that the recorded event occurred

no value dominates: a signed refusal proves a non-action that an egress filter structurally cannot produce, and an external observation proves an effect a refusal cannot; the axis types facts, it does not rank them
unrecognized values fold to unknown, fail-closed: a reader that meets a source class it does not know reports a typed unknown rather than guessing or erroring
